faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Find answers to common questions about our program and university

items.question: Who are we?
items.answer:
We are the Department of Tourism at the State Biotechnological University, part of the Faculty of Trade, Entrepreneurship and Customs Administration. We educate students in the 'Tourism and Recreation' program (specialty 242/J3), preparing them for careers in the tourism industry.
You can learn more about the department here: https://biotechuniv.edu.ua/fakulteti-instituti/fakultet-upravlinnya-torgovelno-pidpriyemnitskoyu-ta-mitnoyu-diyalnistyu/kafedra-turizmu/

items.question: How does the learning process work?
items.answer:
The learning process combines theoretical knowledge with practical training and modern digital tools. Due to the current security situation in city of Kharkiv and its proximity to active conflict zones, the university has implemented an online learning format.
Students attend lectures, complete assignments, and interact with instructors through digital platforms, ensuring continuity and accessibility of education while maintaining academic quality.

items.question: What is the State Biotechnological University?
items.answer:
The State Biotechnological University was established on May 12, 2021, by the Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine (Order No. 431-r), following a proposal from the Ministry of Education and Science.
It was created through the reorganization of four higher education institutions in Kharkiv into a single, powerful university complex. The goal is to provide high-quality education and strengthen scientific potential.
Official website: https://biotechuniv.edu.ua/

items.question: Is the J3 “Tourism and Recreation” program relevant during martial law?
items.answer: Yes, but the focus has shifted. Today, the industry needs specialists who can develop domestic tourism, work with psychological rehabilitation through recreation, and organize safe trips for children and wellness purposes. In addition, we train professionals for Ukraine’s large-scale post-war recovery, where tourism will become one of the key drivers of the economy.

items.question: What careers can I pursue besides being a travel agent?
items.answer: The J3 “Tourism and Recreation” program offers a wide range of career opportunities: hotel and restaurant business manager, territorial development specialist (local communities), tour guide, event manager, specialist in government tourism departments, tourism market analyst, or founder of your own startup.

items.question: Is it possible to specialize in a specific type of tourism (e.g., gastronomic or ecological)?
items.answer: Our elective courses allow you to build your own learning trajectory. You can dive deeper into areas such as gastronomic clusters, wine routes, rural green tourism, or active recreation. We also support course papers and theses focused on topics that interest you the most.

items.question: How will studying at your department help me start my own business?
items.answer: The curriculum includes a block of management disciplines (management, marketing, legal regulation). We teach not only how to work for someone else but also how to develop business plans for tourism startups—from the idea of a unique glamping site to creating your own tour operator.

items.question: Where do students of your department complete their internships?
items.answer: We cooperate with leading hotel complexes in Ukraine (especially in western regions), national natural parks, tour operators, and specialized departments of city councils. There are also international internship programs available (for example, in Turkey, Bulgaria, and Greece).

items.question: How to apply for the Tourism and Recreation program?
items.answer:
To apply for the Tourism and Recreation program, you need to follow these steps:
1. Take the National Multi-Subject Test or Unified Entrance Exam.
2. Register an applicant account based on secondary education or junior bachelor.
3. Submit your documents to the State Biotechnological University.
4. Choose the J3/242 Tourism and Recreation program.
